{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamunazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}}, a {{ill|Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == revision.comment: 37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:加牟那塚古墳 up to rev 101323733
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=加牟那塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]千塚3丁目 |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径45[[メートル|m]]
高さ7.3m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|scale:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=加牟那塚古墳}} |被葬者= |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]片・[[鏡]]・ガラス玉など |史跡指定=山梨県指定史跡「加牟那塚」 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=加牟那塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E}}}} }} '''加牟那塚古墳'''（かんなづかこふん/かむなづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]千塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[山梨県指定文化財一覧|山梨県指定史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「加牟那塚」）{{Cite web|和書|title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡02|publisher=山梨県|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}。 == 立地と地理的・歴史的景観 == 県中部、[[甲府盆地]]の北縁部に位置する。[[奥秩父山地]]の[[金峰山 (山梨県・長野県)|金峰山]]から発し南流する[[荒川 (山梨県)|荒川]]左岸の緩傾斜地に立地し、千塚地区と湯村地区の境界付近にあたる。荒川は[[甲斐市]]との境界を南東に流れ、荒川[[扇状地]]や[[沖積地]]から盆地北縁にかけての一体は[[横穴式石室]]を持つ後期古墳や[[古墳群]]が濃密に分布する。現在は市街化により多くが消失しているが、「千塚（ちづか）」の地名が示す通り[[万寿森古墳]]や[[湯村山]]古墳群など周辺にも多くの古墳が分布し、北部には県内最大級の[[積石塚]]である天狗沢古墳がある。 甲府盆地底部は河川の[[氾濫原]]にあたり安定的定住が困難な地域で、[[弥生時代]]から[[古墳時代]]にかけて遺跡は盆地周辺の丘陵地域や自然堤防、微高地上などに分布している。荒川扇状地では弥生後期の[[榎田遺跡]]があり、近接する[[甲斐市]]南部には弥生中期の[[金の尾遺跡]]が県内で代表的な弥生集落として知られるが、弥生時代から古墳前期までの遺跡は少ない。 弥生後期から古墳前期には盆地南縁の[[曽根丘陵]]に東海地方経由で先進文物を導入し、4世紀後半には[[ヤマト王権]]の影響を受けた[[前方後円墳]]である[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（甲府市下曽根町）や[[岡銚子塚古墳]]（[[笛吹市]]八代地域）などが出現し、古墳中期には中道勢力が衰退し中小規模の古墳築造が盆地各地へ拡大する。盆地北縁では[[山梨県立甲府工業高等学校|塩部遺跡]]や榎田遺跡など[[方形周溝墓]]を伴う5世紀前半から集落遺跡が見られ、6世紀代には横穴式石室を持つ古墳の築造が開始され、[[渡来人]]の進出も見られている。また、荒川右岸地域では[[松ノ尾遺跡]]などこの時期の集落遺跡があり、赤坂台地には[[赤坂台古墳群]]が出現し、加牟那塚古墳はこれら勢力を背景に成立した盟主墳であると考えられている。 [[律令制]]下ではこの地域は[[巨摩郡]]と[[山梨郡]]の境界に近い巨摩郡青沼郷に比定され、荒川右岸地域は松ノ尾遺跡や[[天狗沢瓦窯跡]]から仏教遺物も出土しており、巨摩郡における重要な地域であったと考えられている。6世紀後半から7世紀の甲府盆地では、加牟那塚古墳を中心とする甲府北西部勢力と盆地東部にあたる笛吹市八代町の[[姥塚古墳]]を中心とする御坂勢力の対峙状況が想定されており、加牟那塚古墳や姥塚古墳の存在から両勢力の均衡が古代の郡郷制の確定にも影響していると考えられている。 == 発掘調査と出土遺物・検出遺構 == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient burial mound in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|大正初期頃に撮影された加牟那塚古墳。当時は全面が[[桑畑]]として利用されていた飯田、坂本、小林（1978） p.121。]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 加牟那塚古墳の存在は古くから知られており、[[江戸時代]]には[[1601年]]（[[慶長]]6年）の千塚村検地帳や甲斐国の総合地誌である『[[甲斐国志]]』にも記されており、「金塚」「釜塚」「神塚」などの呼称で呼ばれていた。戦後の[[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）には墳丘裾の補修工事が行われ、工事に先行して[[青山学院大学]]教授の吉田章一郎による[[発掘調査]]が行われている。また、翌[[1970年]]（昭和45年）にも石室入口の修理工事に際して測量調査が行われている。墳丘は二段構成で[[葺石]]が施されており、[[埴輪]]が並ぶ。内部主体は右片袖形の横穴式石室で、真南の方向に開口している。 石室用材が大型化する6世紀後半の古墳で、石室規模では[[笛吹市]]御坂地域の[[姥塚古墳]]に次ぐ。横穴式石室は中国や朝鮮半島に見られる[[墓制]]で、[[単葬墓]]の[[竪穴式石室]]に対し[[家族墓]]であると考えられている。日本には[[4世紀]]末から[[5世紀]]初頭にかけて[[九州]]に出現し、5世紀後半代には畿内においても見られる。[[中部地方]]では5世紀末から出現し、県内では万寿森古墳や荘塚古墳（笛吹市八代地域）が最古で、姥塚や加牟那塚はそれに次ぐ。 出土した[[副葬品]]は[[須恵器]]のほかガラス製小玉のほか、[[内行花文鏡]]、[[神獣鏡]]、[[鼉龍鏡|だ龍鏡]]の三鏡が発見されたと伝わるが、所在が不明。ほか、[[大刀]]や[[盾]]、埴輪類では器財埴輪、馬形埴輪、武人埴輪など。出土した埴輪の胎土分析に拠れば、分析資料11点のうち10点にはほぼ同じ組成で、赤色粒が多く含まれ荒川・塩川の[[河岸段丘]]から採取された[[黒富士]]火山噴出物であると推定されており、[[甲斐型土器]]などの[[土師器]]類とも共通している。1点には[[花崗岩]]類が多く含まれ、曾根丘陵など他地域から持ち込まれたものであるとも考えられている。 出土品の一部は[[山梨県立考古博物館]]に所蔵されている。 == 参考画像 == Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|開口部 Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|墳丘 == 出典 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 坂本美夫「加牟那塚古墳」『山梨県史資料編1考古（遺跡）』 * 坂本美夫「群集墳の出現」『山梨県史通史編1原始・古代』 * 保坂和博「埴輪」『山梨県史資料編2考古（遺物）』 * 河西学「加牟那塚古墳出土埴輪の胎土分析」 * {{Cite book ja-jp | author = [[飯田文彌]]、坂本徳一、小林是綱編著 | year = 1978 | title = 明治大正昭和 甲府|series=故郷の思い出写真集 第10巻 | publisher = [[国書刊行会]]}} == 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Kamunazuka Kofun}} * [[姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)]] * [[万寿森古墳]] {{デフォルトソート:かんなつかこふん}} [[Category:山梨県の古墳]] [[Category:甲府市の歴史]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:山梨県指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[加牟那塚古墳]] to [[Kamunazuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=加牟那塚古墳 |画像=[[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |所在地=[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]千塚3丁目 |形状=[[円墳]] |規模=直径45[[メートル|m]]
高さ7.3m |築造年代=[[6世紀]]後半 |埋葬施設=[[横穴式石室]] |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|scale:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=加牟那塚古墳}} |被葬者= |出土品=[[円筒埴輪]]・[[形象埴輪]]・[[須恵器]]片・[[鏡]]・ガラス玉など |史跡指定=山梨県指定史跡「加牟那塚」 |特記事項= |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Yamanashi|label=加牟那塚
古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=41|lat_sec=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E}}}} }} '''加牟那塚古墳'''（かんなづかこふん/かむなづかこふん）は、[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]千塚にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[円墳]]。[[山梨県指定文化財一覧|山梨県指定史跡]]に指定されている（指定名称は「加牟那塚」）{{Cite web|和書|title=山梨の文化財ガイド（データベース）史跡02|publisher=山梨県|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}。 == 立地と地理的・歴史的景観 == 県中部、[[甲府盆地]]の北縁部に位置する。[[奥秩父山地]]の[[金峰山 (山梨県・長野県)|金峰山]]から発し南流する[[荒川 (山梨県)|荒川]]左岸の緩傾斜地に立地し、千塚地区と湯村地区の境界付近にあたる。荒川は[[甲斐市]]との境界を南東に流れ、荒川[[扇状地]]や[[沖積地]]から盆地北縁にかけての一体は[[横穴式石室]]を持つ後期古墳や[[古墳群]]が濃密に分布する。現在は市街化により多くが消失しているが、「千塚（ちづか）」の地名が示す通り[[万寿森古墳]]や[[湯村山]]古墳群など周辺にも多くの古墳が分布し、北部には県内最大級の[[積石塚]]である天狗沢古墳がある。 弥生後期から古墳前期には盆地南縁の[[曽根丘陵]]に東海地方経由で先進文物を導入し、4世紀後半には[[ヤマト王権]]の影響を受けた[[前方後円墳]]である[[甲斐銚子塚古墳]]（甲府市下曽根町）や[[岡銚子塚古墳]]（[[笛吹市]]八代地域）などが出現し、古墳中期には中道勢力が衰退し中小規模の古墳築造が盆地各地へ拡大する。盆地北縁では[[山梨県立甲府工業高等学校|塩部遺跡]]や榎田遺跡など[[方形周溝墓]]を伴う5世紀前半から集落遺跡が見られ、6世紀代には横穴式石室を持つ古墳の築造が開始され、[[渡来人]]の進出も見られている。また、荒川右岸地域では[[松ノ尾遺跡]]などこの時期の集落遺跡があり、赤坂台地には[[赤坂台古墳群]]が出現し、加牟那塚古墳はこれら勢力を背景に成立した盟主墳であると考えられている。 [[律令制]]下ではこの地域は[[巨摩郡]]と[[山梨郡]]の境界に近い巨摩郡青沼郷に比定され、荒川右岸地域は松ノ尾遺跡や[[天狗沢瓦窯跡]]から仏教遺物も出土しており、巨摩郡における重要な地域であったと考えられている。6世紀後半から7世紀の甲府盆地では、加牟那塚古墳を中心とする甲府北西部勢力と盆地東部にあたる笛吹市八代町の[[姥塚古墳]]を中心とする御坂勢力の対峙状況が想定されており、加牟那塚古墳や姥塚古墳の存在から両勢力の均衡が古代の郡郷制の確定にも影響していると考えられている。 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 出土品の一部は[[山梨県立考古博物館]]に所蔵されている。 == 参考画像 == Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（開口部方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|羨道（開口部方向） Kamunazuka Kofun, Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa}}, fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県}}, Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}, this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror}}, a {{ill|Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a {{ill|Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a {{ill|Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamunazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}, this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}, a {{ill|Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[ja:加牟那塚古墳]]

Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Kofu|ja|甲府市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu|qq=}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|ja|山梨県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefecture|qq=}}, Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō system|qq=}}, this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with {{ill|fukiishi|ja|葺石|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukiishi|qq=}} and decorated with {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/haniwa|qq=}}. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}, small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an {{ill|Inner Arc Mirror|ja|内行花文鏡|1=Flower mirror|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Arc Mirror|13=User:Immanuelle/Flower mirror|qq=}}, a {{ill|Shinju-kyo|lt=Shinju-kyo|lt=Divine Beast Mirror|ja|神獣鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}}, and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[ja:加牟那塚古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]]

It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[ja:加牟那塚古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines.
