revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamunazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|加牟那塚古墳|version=101323733|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun template and pages that transclude it
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamunazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|加牟那塚古墳|version=101323733|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[ja:加牟那塚古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] {{Commons category|Kamunazuka Kofun}}

Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35|41|04.51|N|138|32|37.01|E|map_size:5000_region:JP-19_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Kamunazuka Kofun}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Figural Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Figural Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An Kamunazuka ancient Kofun in 1910 's.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|加牟那塚古墳|version=101323733|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[sn:Kamunazuka Kofun]] [[ja:加牟那塚古墳]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun of Yamanashi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Yamanashi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kofu, Yamanashi]] {{Commons category|Kamunazuka Kofun}}

Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.684586|N|138.543614|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|qq=|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|加牟那塚古墳|version=101323733|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.684586|N|138.543614|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods [[sn:commons:Category:Kamunazuka Kofun]][[ja:加牟那塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11398722]] Wikidata name: Kamunazuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kamunazuka Kofun | image = [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun and stone chamber entrance | location = [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka 3-chome | type = {{ill|Round tumulus|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round tumulus|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | area = Diameter: 45m
Height: 7.3m | period = Late 6th century | burial = {{ill|Corridor-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.684586|N|138.543614|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | burial_individual = | burial goods = {{ill|Cylindrical haniwa|ja|円筒埴輪|1=Cylindrical Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Cylindrical Haniwa|qq=}}, {{ill|Figurative haniwa|ja|形象埴輪|1=Representational Haniwa|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Figurative haniwa|13=User:Immanuelle/Representational Haniwa|qq=}}, fragments of [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], mirrors, glass beads, etc. | Historic Site = Yamanashi Prefecture Designated Historic Site | map = {{location map|Japan Yamanashi|map_dot_label=Kamunazuka
Kofun|coordinates=35|coordinates=41|coordinates=04.51|lon_deg=138|lon_min=32|lon_sec=37.01|marksize=10|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=}} | architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Kamunazuka Kofun''' (加牟那塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Kōfu|Kofu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]], Chizuka. It has been Historic Site1 a Yamanashi Prefecture Historic Site under the name "Kamunazuka"{{Cite web|title=Yamanashi designation1 Guide (Database) Historic Sites 02|publisher=Yamanashi Prefecture|url=https://www.pref.yamanashi.jp/bunka/maibun/yamanashinobunkazai_shiseki002.html#SHI0019|accessdate=2020-05-11}}. == location and Geographic-Historical Landscape == The tumulus is situated in central Yamanashi, at the northern edge of the {{ill|Kōfu Basin|lt=Kōfu Basin|lt=Kofu Basin|ja|甲府盆地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofu Basin|qq=}}. It lies on the gentle slopes of the left bank of the {{ill|Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|ja|荒川 (山梨県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arakawa River (Yamanashi)|qq=}}, near the boundary between Chizuka and Yumura districts. The area is part of the region known for its dense distribution of late-period kofun with {{ill|corridor-style stone chambers|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/corridor-style stone chambers|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, though many have been lost to urbanization. Nearby burial sites include the {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} and the Yumurayama Kofun Cluster, as well as the Tengu-sawa Kofun, the largest stone-piled mound in Yamanashi Prefecture. The region’s toponym "Chizuka" reflects this legacy{{Cite book|title=Yamanashi Prefecture Place names|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1995|page=357}}. During the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]], this area was near the boundary of the {{ill|Koma District|ja|巨摩郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koma District|qq=}} and {{ill|Yamanashi District|ja|山梨郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi District|qq=}}. Archaeological evidence from sites like the Matsunoo Ruins suggests that this region was significant in the spread of Buddhism and political power during the late 6th to early 7th century. == Excavation and burial goods == [[File:An_Kamunazuka_ancient_burial_mound_in_1910_%27s.jpg|thumb|240px|Kamunazuka Kofun in the early Taisho period.]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kamunazuka Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Layout of the stone chamber}}]] Kamunazuka Kofun has been known since the Edo period. It is mentioned in the 1601 survey records for Chizuka Village and the comprehensive regional history *{{ill|Kai Kokushi|ja|甲斐国志|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kai Kokushi|1=Kaikokushi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11577871}}*, under various names such as "Kanazuka," "Kamazuka," and "Kamizuka." Excavations in 1969, conducted by Professor Shoichiro Yoshida of Aoyama Gakuin University, revealed the tumulus's two-tiered structure, with a diameter of 45 meters and a height of 7.3 meters. The mound was covered with [[:en:Fukiishi|fukiishi]] and decorated with [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]]. The burial facility is a south-facing, single-sleeve corridor-style stone chamber built with large stone materials indicative of the late 6th century. burial goods found include [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], small glass beads, and three mirrors, identified as an [[:en:Flower mirror|Flower mirror]], a [[:en:Shinju-kyo|Divine Beast Mirror]], and a {{ill|Daryu Mirror|ja|鼉龍鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daryu Mirror|qq=}}. However, the location of these mirrors is currently unknown. Other finds include swords, shields, and various haniwa types, such as ceremonial implements, horse figurines, and warrior figurines. Soil analysis of the haniwa revealed that most were made from volcanic materials sourced from nearby rivers, while some included granite, likely imported from regions like the Sone Hills. burial goods from the kofun are housed in the {{ill|Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Kai Fudoki Hill|lt=Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja|山梨県立考古博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamanashi Prefectural Archaeological Museum|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 曽根丘陵公園}}. == Reference images == File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward rear wall) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-2.jpg|Passage (view toward entrance) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Passage (view toward main chamber) File:Kamunazuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance File:Kamunazuka Kofun, funkyu-2.jpg|Kofun == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Yoshio Sakamoto, "Kamunazuka Kofun," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 1: Archaeology (Sites)*. * Yoshio Sakamoto, "The Emergence of burial Clusters," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Overview Vol. 1: Ancient and Early Modern History*. * Kazuhiro Hosaka, "Haniwa," *Yamanashi Prefectural Historical Records Vol. 2: Archaeology (burial goods)*. * Manabu Kawanishi, "Clay Analysis of Haniwa from Kamunazuka Kofun." * {{Cite book|author=Fumiya Iida, Tokuichi Sakamoto, Koretsuna Kobayashi|year=1978|title=Meiji, Taisho, Showa: Kofu|series=Hometown Memories Photo Collection Vol. 10|publisher=Kokusho Kankokai}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Ubazuka Kofun|ja|姥塚古墳 (笛吹市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ubazuka Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|Manjumori Kofun|ja|万寿森古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjumori Kofun|qq=}} * {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated
